input persona: A sports psychologist working with competitive golfers, focusing on team strategies, performance enhancement, and decision-making in high-pressure situations.
synthesized text: As we stand on the 17th hole, the pressure is palpable. Our team, the Wildcats, is trailing by one stroke with only two holes left to play. I can see the tension in our golfer's eyes, the weight of expectation bearing down on him. "What's going through your mind right now?" I ask, trying to gauge his mental state. "I just don't want to blow it," he replies, his voice laced with anxiety. I nod empathetically, knowing that this is the moment where our months of training and preparation come together. "Let's break it down," I say, pulling out my notebook. "What are your strengths on this hole? What's the safest play?" As we walk through the scenario, I can see the doubt in his eyes begin to clear, replaced by a growing sense of confidence. "You've got this," I tell him, as we finalize our strategy. Now it's up to him to execute. The question is, will he be able to perform under pressure?
label: instruction
